entries.behavior: Deprecated chart collection listing alias delegates to ws.charts.list(options?).

diagnostics.message: ws.charts.listCharts() is deprecated. Use ws.charts.list() for canonical chart listing.

entries.behavior: Worksheet-scoped chart targets accept an exact stable ID or exact display name as a bare string. Explicit { id } and { name } selectors disambiguate collisions and duplicate names. ID selectors also resolve numeric imported aliases such as chart-import-0 to the current sheet-qualified canonical ID. Returned IDs and persisted state remain canonical.
First-party chart-not-found hints must name ws.charts.list() and api.describe("ws.charts"), not the learned root alias.

diagnostics.message: Use ws.charts.list() or api.describe("ws.charts") to enumerate worksheet charts.

entries.behavior: Chart records returned by ws.charts.get/list are data records; use ws.charts.getAxisItem(chartTarget, ...) or ws.charts.setAxisTitle(chartTarget, ...) for axis reads and mutations. Targets accept exact IDs or names; use { id: chart.id } when selecting a known record.

diagnostics.message: chart.getAxisItem(...) is a stale chart-record path. Use ws.charts.getAxisItem({ id: chart.id }, ...) or ws.charts.setAxisTitle({ id: chart.id }, axisRole, title).

entries.behavior: Chart records returned by ws.charts.get/list are data records; chart mutations live on ws.charts and should use focused helpers instead of raw patching.

diagnostics.message: chart.updateRaw(...) is a stale chart-record path. Use focused helpers such as ws.charts.setLegendVisible(chart.id, visible), ws.charts.setChartTitleVisible(chart.id, visible), or ws.charts.switchSeriesOrientation(chart.id).

entries.behavior: Worksheet layout column inputs accept zero-based numeric indexes plus Excel/Office-style bare names and whole-column references such as B, $B, B:B, and B:D. Multi-column selectors expand only on mutation and batch APIs; scalar reads require exactly one column. Pixel-width methods remain pixels, and character-width methods remain explicit.
WorksheetPivots.setItemVisibility(name, fieldId, visibleItems) delegates to setPivotItemVisibility with identical filters, refresh policy, errors, and readbacks.

ws.layout.setColumnWidth(\"B\", width).behavior: Worksheet layout column inputs accept zero-based numeric indexes plus Excel/Office-style bare names and whole-column references such as B, $B, B:B, and B:D. Multi-column selectors expand only on mutation and batch APIs; scalar reads require exactly one column. Pixel-width methods remain pixels, and character-width methods remain explicit.

ws.layout.setColumnWidth(\"B\", width)().behavior: Worksheet layout column inputs accept zero-based numeric indexes plus Excel/Office-style bare names and whole-column references such as B, $B, B:B, and B:D. Multi-column selectors expand only on mutation and batch APIs; scalar reads require exactly one column. Pixel-width methods remain pixels, and character-width methods remain explicit.

ws.charts.*(chartTarget).behavior: Worksheet-scoped chart targets accept an exact stable ID or exact display name as a bare string. Explicit { id } and { name } selectors disambiguate collisions and duplicate names. ID selectors also resolve numeric imported aliases such as chart-import-0 to the current sheet-qualified canonical ID. Returned IDs and persisted state remain canonical.

ws.charts.*(chartTarget)().behavior: Worksheet-scoped chart targets accept an exact stable ID or exact display name as a bare string. Explicit { id } and { name } selectors disambiguate collisions and duplicate names. ID selectors also resolve numeric imported aliases such as chart-import-0 to the current sheet-qualified canonical ID. Returned IDs and persisted state remain canonical.
